$description: Intentional substring relationships between aimock fixture userMessages. Every entry is a deliberate ordering decision (e.g. CrewAI startup probe matches a longer prefix before falling through to a generic short fixture, or a multi-framework fixture set keeps short broad-match keys as fallbacks for legacy tests). Adding entries here is an escape hatch — prefer fixing the fixture's userMessage to be more specific. The fixture-overlap test consumes this list. d5-all.json is loaded BEFORE feature-parity.json, so d5-all.json fixtures take precedence at runtime when both would match.

entries.reason: Pre-existing legacy multi-framework greeting fallback in feature-parity.json. The bare 'hi' fixture acts as a broad catch-all for greeting demos in older showcase packages. Canonical demos use longer, distinctive prompts so they don't bleed into 'hi'.
Pre-existing legacy multi-framework greeting fallback in feature-parity.json. Same rationale as 'hi'.
Pre-existing legacy multi-framework greeting fallback in feature-parity.json. Capitalized variant kept for case-sensitive frameworks.
Pre-existing legacy multi-framework help-prompt fallback in feature-parity.json. Returns the demo capability summary when a user types 'help'.

Pre-existing greeting demo prompts (popup/sidebar) live in d5-all.json which loads before feature-parity.json, so they match first. The bare 'hi' in feature-parity is a legacy multi-framework greeting fallback.

Pre-existing legacy multi-framework fallback ordering. 'hi' is a generic greeting fallback and 'this PDF' is a multimodal probe; both intentional in feature-parity.json.
